# Terminal Demo

This walkthrough shows the MVP flow: validate policy, compile OpenSSH artifacts, evaluate access, and generate a safe certificate issuance plan.

```bash
$ accessc check examples/policy.yaml
policy ok: 2 rule(s)
```

```bash
$ accessc compile examples/policy.yaml --out dist
compiled OpenSSH artifacts to dist
```

Generated server snippet:

```conf
# dist/sshd/sshd_config.snippet
# Generated by accessc
TrustedUserCAKeys /etc/ssh/accessc_ca.pub
```

```bash
$ accessc decide examples/policy.yaml --principal user:alice --action ssh --resource server:prod --ttl 5m --ssh-principal alice
allow: rule=allow-alice-prod ttl=300s
```

```bash
$ accessc plan examples/policy.yaml --principal user:alice --resource server:prod --ttl 5m --ssh-principal alice --out dist
planned OpenSSH certificate issuance to dist
```

Generated issue command:

```bash
# dist/ssh/issue-command.txt
ssh-keygen -s /path/to/ca_key -I accessc-user-alice-server-prod-alice -n alice -V +300s /path/to/user.pub
```

Generated audit receipt shape:

```json
{
  "event": "certificate_issuance_planned",
  "decision": "allow",
  "plan_id": "user-alice-server-prod-alice",
  "principal": "user:alice",
  "action": "ssh",
  "resource": "server:prod",
  "ssh_principal": "alice",
  "ttl_seconds": 300,
  "matched_rule": "allow-alice-prod"
}
```

Denied requests exit with code `2` and print the matching deny rule:

```bash
$ accessc decide examples/policy.yaml --principal user:alice --action ssh --resource server:prod --ttl 5m --ssh-principal root
deny: rule=deny-prod-root
```
